name: Blinded
description: A blinded creature cannot see, fails sight-based checks, attacks poorly, and is easier to hit.
effects: Blindsight, tremorsense, or other senses may change practical impact.
Use for darkness, eye injuries, magical blindness, or sensory denial.

name: Charmed
description: A charmed creature cannot harm the charmer and the charmer has social leverage over it.
effects: Charmed is not mind control unless the effect says so.
Use to limit hostility without taking full control of the creature.

name: Deafened
description: A deafened creature cannot hear and fails checks that require hearing.
effects: Being deafened does not stop verbal components by itself.
Use for thunderous effects, silence-like results, or damaged hearing.

name: Exhausted
effects: Track the exact level and apply all lower-level effects too; long rests remove only what the rule allows.

name: Frightened
description: A frightened creature has trouble acting while the fear source is in sight and cannot willingly move closer to it.
effects: Breaking line of sight can remove the disadvantage but not necessarily the condition.
Use to control movement and pressure attacks without paralyzing the creature.

name: Grappled
description: A grappled creature has Speed 0 and cannot benefit from speed bonuses.
effects: Grappled is not restrained unless another rule says so.
Forced movement can break the grapple if it separates reach.
Use to pin a creature in place, set up hazards, or stop movement.

name: Incapacitated
description: An incapacitated creature cannot take actions or reactions; 2024 also calls out bonus actions and concentration more explicitly.
effects: Many other conditions include incapacitated.
Check edition wording for concentration and speech details.
Use when a creature is stunned, paralyzed, unconscious, or otherwise unable to act.

name: Invisible
description: An invisible creature cannot be seen normally, is harder to attack, and attacks with advantage unless a special sense counters it.
effects: Invisible is not the same as undetected.
Blindsight and similar senses can reduce or remove the benefit.
Use special senses, tracks, noise, and search checks to adjudicate whether enemies know the creature's location.

name: Paralyzed
description: A paralyzed creature is incapacitated, cannot move, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, and is vulnerable to close critical hits.
effects: Close melee hits are automatic critical hits if they hit.
The creature still occupies space and can be targeted.
Use for hold-person style effects and high-danger lockdown.

name: Petrified
description: A petrified creature becomes an inert solid substance, is incapacitated, cannot move, and is much harder but helpless.
effects: Check the exact spell or monster effect for saving throws and reversal.
Use as a severe battlefield removal effect with rescue potential.

name: Poisoned
description: A poisoned creature has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
effects: Poison damage and the poisoned condition are separate.
Immunity to poison damage may or may not imply immunity to the condition depending on stat block wording.
Use as a simple debuff that pressures attackers and skill users.

name: Prone
description: A prone creature crawls or spends movement to stand, attacks at disadvantage, and is easier to hit in melee.
effects: Standing costs movement, not an action.
A flying creature knocked prone may fall unless it can hover or has a rule preventing the fall.
Use for trips, falls, shove effects, and low-position tactics.

name: Restrained
description: A restrained creature has Speed 0, attacks poorly, is easier to hit, and has disadvantage on Dexterity saves.
effects: Restrained is stronger than grappled.
Forced movement may not end restrained unless the effect says so.
Use for webs, nets, vines, tentacles, and binding effects.

name: Stunned
description: A stunned creature is incapacitated, cannot move, has impaired speech, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, and is easier to hit.
effects: A stunned creature usually cannot take reactions.
Many stun effects repeat saves at end of turn.
Use as a high-impact control condition; track duration carefully.

name: Unconscious
description: An unconscious creature is incapacitated, prone, unaware, drops held items, and is highly vulnerable to nearby attacks.
effects: A stable unconscious creature at 0 HP remains unconscious until healed or time passes.
Melee hits can rapidly cause death-save failures.
Use for sleep, knockout, and 0 hit point states when applicable.
